text:
Adam Peatyhas slammed the catering at the Olympic Village and claims athletes are finding ‘worms’ in their food.

Olympicsorganisers have faced widespread criticism from athletes staying at the village in Paris, with complaints ranging from the uncomfortable beds which require mattress toppers,to the high temperate of the rooms due to the lack of air conditioning.

Several teams, including theUnited Statesand India, have taken matters into their own hands by shipping in portable air-conditioning units to help athletes deal with the heat in the rooms.

However, there appears to have been little improvement in the level of the catering after Team GB’s chief executive, Andy Anson,revealed two weeks ago that athletes had been served ‘raw meat’.

And Peaty, who won a silver medal in the men’s 100m breaststroke in Paris, claims the standard of the food has fallen well short of what athletes need.

‘The catering isn’t good enough for the level the athletes are expected to perform. We need to give the best we possibly can,’ Peaty said in an interview withthe i newspaper.

‘Tokyo, the food was incredible, Rio was incredible. But this time around… there wasn’t enough protein options, long queues, waiting 30 minutes for food because there’s no queuing system.

‘These [complaints] are for people to get better. And the organising committee, so we’ll put these back to our team in full depth and detail.

‘But it’s definitely been the best Games in terms of fans engaged with it. So you never gonna have a perfect Games.’

Paris 2024’s organising committee have aimed to cut the average carbon emissions per meal in half compared to previous Olympics by offering more vegetarian food.

However, Peaty believes athletes should not be forced to change their diet during the competition.

‘The narrative of sustainability has just been punished on the athletes,’ Peaty said.

‘I want to eat meat, I need meat to perform and that’s what I eat at home, so why should I change?

‘I like my fish and people are finding worms in the fish. It’s just not good enough.

‘The standard, we’re looking at the best of the best in the world, and we’re feeding them not the best.

‘I just want people to get better at their roles and jobs. And I think that’s what the athletes are the best sounding board for.’

title: Adam Peaty reveals grim Olympic Village catering; says 'worms' are found in food | Metro News
description: Adam Peaty has slammed the catering at the Olympic village and claims athletes are finding 'worms' in their food.
